recommendations.summary: Current solution remains the best fit. While mxbai-embed-large shows promise with strong MTEB scores, it lacks MoE efficiency and the migration cost to re-index all collections isn't justified for incremental gains.

findings.name: mxbai-embed-large
findings.description: State-of-the-art embedding model from mixedbread.ai with excellent MTEB performance, widely recommended for RAG applications. However, it's a dense model (335M params) without MoE architecture.

findings.name: nomic-embed-text-v1.5
findings.description: Newer version of Nomic embeddings with Matryoshka support and resizable dimensions. Claims performance better than OpenAI models except text-embedding-3-large.

findings.name: BGE-M3
findings.description: Strong multilingual embedding model with 63.0 MTEB score, MIT licensed and self-hostable. Good privacy-focused alternative but unclear Ollama availability.

recommendations.summary: Qwen3-Coder shows significant improvements in agentic workflows and code quality, but GLM-4.7-Flash might offer better performance per VRAM GB.

findings.name: Qwen3-Coder
findings.description: Latest iteration with agentic coding capabilities, supporting 256K+ context and 100+ programming languages. Multiple sources indicate significant improvements over Qwen2.5.

findings.name: GLM-4.7-Flash (Reasoning)
findings.description: Dominates coding benchmarks with 25.9 Coding Index, significantly ahead of 30B models while using less VRAM. Won agentic coding challenge by building procedural rendering engine.

findings.name: DeepSeek-Coder-V3.2-Exp
findings.description: Experimental reasoning model approaching O3/Gemini 2.5 Pro performance levels, representing cutting-edge advancement in AI reasoning for code.

recommendations.summary: Several distilled reasoning models have emerged that offer improved performance while maintaining 24GB VRAM compatibility. DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen3-8B and GLM-4.7 show particularly strong results.

findings.name: DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen3-8B
findings.description: Distilled version of DeepSeek-R1 that reportedly matches 235B models on reasoning tasks while running on single GPU. Achieves 87.5% on AIME 2025 benchmark.

findings.name: GLM-4.7
findings.description: MoE model (~355B/32B active) that leads open-source models across reasoning, coding, and agent tasks. Scores 42.8% on Humanity's Last Exam benchmark with tool use.

findings.name: Kimi K2 Thinking
findings.description: MoE model with exceptional deep reasoning and long-horizon tool use. Features INT4 efficiency optimizations and 256K context length.

findings.name: DeepSeek V3.2
findings.description: Latest evolution showing improved performance approaching O3 and Gemini 2.5 Pro levels. May be too large for 24GB constraint.

recommendations.summary: Qwen2.5:7b remains solid, but newer alternatives like Phi-4-mini (3.8B) and Qwen3:4b offer better VRAM efficiency with potentially comparable performance for lightweight tasks.

findings.name: Phi-4-mini
findings.description: Microsoft's 3.8B parameter model with 67.3% benchmark performance and 128K context length, requiring only ~3GB VRAM. Appears well-optimized for instruction following.

findings.name: Qwen3:4b
findings.description: Next generation 4B parameter Qwen model with 59.6% benchmark performance and 128K context. Natural upgrade path from current Qwen2.5 stack.

findings.name: Gemma 3:4b
findings.description: Google's 4B parameter model with 59.6% performance and 128K context length, requiring ~3GB VRAM. Available through Ollama.

recommendations.current: Anthropic Claude (via LiteLLM) + Google Gemini (via LiteLLM)
recommendations.summary: Current combination remains optimal. Claude provides excellent reasoning quality and structured output, while Gemini's production-ready models offer cost-effective background processing.

findings.name: Gemini 1.5 Pro-002 & Flash-002
findings.description: Updated production-ready Gemini models with improved performance and higher rate limits (1,000 RPM for Pro, 2,000 RPM for Flash). Now fully GA status.

findings.name: Gemini 2.5 models
findings.description: Production-ready next-generation models positioned for enterprise use, offering both premium reasoning and cost-effective variants.

findings.name: Claude Opus 4.5
findings.description: Mentioned as Anthropic's flagship model in 2026, though specific details about availability and improvements unclear from search results.

recommendations.summary: Qdrant remains the best choice. No alternatives provide meaningful advantages for this use case while justifying the high migration cost of rebuilding three established collections.

findings.name: pgvector/PostgreSQL
findings.description: Could consolidate with relational data if needed, but offers no vector search advantages over Qdrant and would require significant schema redesign.

findings.name: Weaviate
findings.description: Strong hybrid search capabilities, but Qdrant already handles payload filtering well and hybrid search isn't a critical gap for current workflows.

findings.name: Milvus
findings.description: Performance-optimized for massive scale, but current usage doesn't require billion-vector scale and setup complexity is higher than Qdrant.

recommendations.summary: LiteLLM continues to satisfy all hard constraints effectively. While alternatives exist, none offer compelling advantages that justify medium-high migration costs.

findings.name: Bifrost by Maxim AI
findings.description: Go-based gateway claiming 50x performance improvement over LiteLLM with enterprise governance features, but appears to be a commercial offering despite 'open-source' claims.

findings.name: TrueFoundry
findings.description: Enterprise-focused platform with full LLM lifecycle management, but significantly more complex than needed for a simple gateway role.

findings.name: Kong AI Gateway
findings.description: Enterprise API gateway with AI-specific features, but overkill for current requirements and lacks the lightweight proxy approach needed.

recommendations.current: Langfuse v3 (Docker, web + worker + clickhouse + redis + minio)
recommendations.summary: Langfuse remains the best fit. All mentioned alternatives are managed services incompatible with self-hosting requirement, and operator constraints mandate routing all traces to Langfuse specifically.

findings.name: Braintrust
findings.description: Managed platform with CI/CD blocking but requires managed infrastructure, violating self-hosting constraint.

findings.name: LangWatch
findings.description: Comprehensive monitoring platform but appears to be managed/SaaS only, not self-hostable.

findings.name: Opik
findings.description: Open-source option with 7-14x performance claims and automated optimization, but lacks clarity on OpenTelemetry support.

recommendations.summary: PydanticAI remains the best fit for the operator's constraints. No alternatives better address the specific need for structured output + LiteLLM integration with minimal abstraction.

findings.name: LangChain 1.0
findings.description: Major Python framework with extensive ecosystem. However, known for heavy abstraction layers which violates the operator's preference for minimal abstraction.

findings.name: Google ADK (Agent Development Kit)
findings.description: New framework from Google with hierarchical agent compositions. Still early stage and may not have mature LiteLLM integration.

findings.name: CrewAI
findings.description: Multi-agent framework with 30k+ GitHub stars. Focused on collaborative agents but may be overkill for single-shot agents with tools.

recommendations.summary: Tailscale remains the optimal choice for zero-config mesh VPN with WireGuard. Alternatives require significant trade-offs in ease-of-use or additional infrastructure overhead.

findings.name: Headscale
findings.description: Open source Tailscale control server implementation. Provides exact Tailscale client compatibility but requires self-hosting the coordination server.

findings.name: NetBird
findings.description: Fully open source mesh VPN with modern web interface, SSO integration, and built-in DNS management. Requires client installation and self-hosted infrastructure.

findings.name: Cloudflare Zero Trust Tunnels
findings.description: Cloudflare-hosted tunneling solution that eliminates VPN clients entirely. HTTP/HTTPS only, not suitable for general mesh networking or localhost SSH access.

recommendations.summary: ntfy remains the optimal choice for this use case - it directly satisfies all hard constraints and most soft preferences with proven stability and minimal resource usage.

findings.name: Gotify
findings.description: Alternative self-hosted push notification server. However, Android-only (no iOS client) and more resource-heavy than ntfy.

findings.name: Pushover
findings.description: Commercial service, violates self-hosted constraint despite being mentioned as ntfy alternative.

recommendations.current: Obsidian Headless Sync (ob-sync CLI, systemd service)
recommendations.summary: Official Obsidian headless client released 2026-02-27, but appears to be the same 'ob' CLI you're already using. Current setup is optimal.

findings.name: Official Obsidian Headless Client
findings.description: Obsidian released official headless sync client 2026-02-27 using 'ob' command - matches your current ob-sync CLI implementation

findings.name: obsidian-headless npm package
findings.description: Beta headless client installable via npm, but appears to be same underlying 'ob' CLI tool with different distribution method

recommendations.summary: rclone remains the dominant solution with no compelling alternatives emerging. The search results confirm it as the de facto standard.

findings.name: Insync
findings.description: Commercial GUI client with selective sync and multiple account support, but adds complexity and licensing cost for equivalent functionality.

findings.name: Syncovery
findings.description: Advanced sync tool with complex profiles and filters, overkill for simple Google Drive sync with per-device licensing costs.

recommendations.summary: n8n remains the optimal choice - strong Telegram integration, mature ecosystem, and visual editor align with operator's ADHD/autism accommodations. No alternatives found with compelling advantages to justify migration costs.

findings.name: Activepieces
findings.description: MIT-licensed visual workflow builder with cleaner interface than n8n. Emerging alternative but lacks n8n's established ecosystem and Telegram integration maturity.

findings.name: Windmill.dev
findings.description: High-performance developer platform for script-to-workflow conversion. Better for code-first teams but would require abandoning visual workflow paradigm.

findings.name: Bit Flows
findings.description: WordPress-focused automation tool. Not relevant for this use case which centers on Telegram bot workflows and LiteLLM integration.

recommendations.summary: uv remains the optimal choice - it's performant, satisfies all hard constraints, and has become the industry standard. No compelling alternatives found.

findings.name: Poetry
findings.description: Comprehensive project management tool with integrated packaging and publishing, but benchmarks show 3x slower than uv on cold installs and lock file generation.

findings.name: pip-tools
findings.description: Minimal learning curve and generates standard requirements.txt format, but lacks the comprehensive project management and speed advantages of uv.

findings.name: PDM
findings.description: Lightweight alternative mentioned in comparisons, but no compelling advantages over uv found in search results.

recommendations.current: MCP (Model Context Protocol) with multiple servers
recommendations.summary: MCP remains the standard for Claude Code integration. The search reveals an expanding ecosystem of servers rather than replacement protocols.

findings.name: Composio MCP
findings.description: Library offering 100+ pre-built MCP servers for common integrations like GitHub, Jira, databases. Could reduce custom server development.

findings.name: Appwrite MCP Server
findings.description: Official MCP server for Appwrite backend services, enabling AI to manage databases and users through prompts.

findings.name: Neon MCP Server
findings.description: Connects AI clients directly to Postgres databases via MCP, useful for database queries and schema exploration.

findings.name: GitHub MCP Server
findings.description: Enables AI to fetch repo data, review PRs, and create issues. Multiple implementations available in the ecosystem.

findings.name: MBro Terminal Client
findings.description: Interactive terminal MCP browser with tab completion for working with multiple servers and managing complex workflows.

recommendations.summary: Docling remains the best open-source parsing choice. Search reveals alternative frameworks but no compelling direct replacements for document parsing itself.

findings.name: LlamaParse
findings.description: LlamaIndex's proprietary parsing service specifically designed for RAG with excellent layout understanding, but commercial service with API costs.

findings.name: Unstructured.io
findings.description: Popular open-source parsing library with good format support and chunking strategies, though less sophisticated layout analysis than Docling.

findings.name: Haystack document processors
findings.description: Complete RAG framework with integrated parsing and chunking, but heavier architectural commitment than standalone parsing.
